---
title: "Instructor Only: Flow And Attrition Practice"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath("../../../.."))
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Worked Reference

```{r}
nhanes <- readr::read_csv("data/real/nhanes_2021_2023_analysis.csv")

attrition <- data.frame(
  stage = c(
    "All records",
    "Adults",
    "Adults with BP",
    "Adults with BP and diet",
    "Adults with BP, diet, and income"
  ),
  n = c(
    nrow(nhanes),
    nhanes |> filter(age_years >= 18) |> nrow(),
    nhanes |> filter(age_years >= 18, !is.na(systolic_mean)) |> nrow(),
    nhanes |> filter(age_years >= 18, !is.na(systolic_mean), !is.na(mean_daily_energy_kcal)) |> nrow(),
    nhanes |> filter(age_years >= 18, !is.na(systolic_mean), !is.na(mean_daily_energy_kcal), !is.na(family_income_to_poverty)) |> nrow()
  )
)

attrition <- attrition |>
  mutate(stage = factor(stage, levels = stage), retained_percent = n / first(n) * 100)

ggplot(attrition, aes(x = stage, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(retained_percent), "% retained")), hjust = -0.05) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Attrition Across Analysis Filters",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of rows"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
